spec.emotional_state: hopeful, made some progress on a goal
spec.label: A02_same_day

profile.personality: curious but reserved; thinks before replying; warms up after a few turns

transcript.content: Hi Priyansh, the India vs Australia match today is on, what's your call on who wins?
India will win, I'm sure. Australia is good but Kohli and Jasprit are in good form. You follow cricket?
"India's got a strong team, Kohli and Jasprit make a great combo. I follow cricket, but not as closely as you, what position do you like in a match?"
I like batting. If you bat well, you win 70% matches. I also play some local games. My favorite actor is Shah Rukh Khan. You watch Bollywood?
India's got a strong team, Kohli and Jasprit make a great combo. I follow cricket, but not as closely as you, what position do you like in a match?
Batsman. I try to score fast. We also play gully cricket sometimes. You like any sport?
Gully cricket sounds fun! Batsmen who score fast are exciting to watch. I enjoy cricket most when it's close, what's your take on tight finishes?
Tight finishes are the best! You get so much adrenalin. Last ball wins, that's most thrilling. I also like underdog stories. Which one you like more?
That sounds exciting! Underdog stories are amazing when they win against all odds. Do you have a favourite underdog match or movie?
Yes, one match and one movie. The 2004 semi-final between India and Sri Lanka, Sri Lanka won with last ball. And movie is "Dil Chahta Hai" — it's underdog in its own way. Both are special.
That’s a beautiful pick, Sri Lanka’s win and *Dil Chahta Hai* both show heart. What part of the movie made it feel like an underdog to you?
The movie didn’t follow typical Bollywood formula, more realistic and raw. It was not hyped much but became a cult classic. That made it feel underdog.
I get that, raw stories stand out when they don’t chase trends. What made you watch it the first time?
A friend recommended it. He said it’s different. I watched it and really connected with the characters. It was not like other movies I was used to. From that day, it became my favorite.
That sounds like a great recommendation. Friends are the best for finding hidden gems. Any other movies you’ve connected with like that?

moments.text: I love when a match ends with a tight finish, like a last-ball win. It gives me adrenaline.
I remember one match where India chased 300 in the last over with friends, we were all shouting. It's moments like that.
